title: New Feature: Subitem Filters!
excerpt:
New Feature Alert!
We have a big announcement that many have been waiting for… 
Are you ready?! 
We are gradually releasing subitem filters! With this releases, subitems can be filtered by person, status, due date, and m…

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
New Feature Alert!

We have a big announcement that many have been waiting for…

Are you ready?!

We are gradually releasing subitem filters! With this releases, subitems can be filtered by person, status, due date, and more! Keep an lookout for this new feature and let us know your thoughts

posts.author: DanLiebeschutz (Dan Liebeschutz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Waiting a long time for this feature

Thank you!

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: Awesome. Will this also allow the opportunity for Filtered results to includ both items and subitems?

posts.author: mstew1110 (Morgan)
posts.cookedHtml: The “Show Summary on Parent Item” still does not work for the filters. It still shows the total aggregation of all subitems and not just the filtered items, when will this be fixed?

posts.author: mdavis (Melissa Davis)
posts.cookedHtml: How exciting!!! I’m glad the subitems have more functionality now, keep them coming!

posts.author: StrictLine (StrictLine)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve already used this for longer, maybe this feature has been rolled out seemlessly, but the subitems does not get filtered from the items, so those items shows up, which has a subitem with the given filter value.

Furthermore, it’d be nice to have the option in GraphQL.

posts.author: mstew1110 (Morgan)
posts.cookedHtml: Exports also DO NOT work with the subitem filter - this is strictly a view only filter and is utterly worthless until it is released in full (which none of Monday.com’s releases seem to ever do).

posts.author: j.truong (Jennifer Truong)
posts.cookedHtml: I can see the subitem filters, but I don’t think they work for deadline mode right now. I want to filter subitems that are considered overdue, but I don’t see the option for subitem timelines. I hope you can incorporate deadline mode with the subitem filters, but in the meantime, thank you for your hard work so far!

posts.author: frossini (Fab Rossini)
posts.cookedHtml:
Awesome! @Julietteb
But it does not seem to have been deployed in the table widget… I wanted to have a dashboard view with all the files of the project and filter all the items with a links in the table. And the links are in the subitems. Cannot do it. The content of the subitem is not available in the filters. Will this be release soon?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey community!

Just checked with the team. To confirm, filter subitems in dashboards gradually be released in the next few weeks. For now, you may only see the capability of filtering subitems in boards.

For more info, check out this article about filtering subitems!

posts.author: sswale (Sandra Swale)
posts.cookedHtml: The sub item “HIDE” does not show the sub item list for views. This is really important for us.

posts.author: KenanSeba (Kenan)
posts.cookedHtml: filtering subimtems still does not work within Dashboards and Widgets!

posts.author: CJandCJ (Caleb)
posts.cookedHtml: Awesome! Thank you! =D

posts.author: mam7903 (Mike Mattingly)
posts.cookedHtml:
This only filters items with subitems correctly… it leaves out all items WITHOUT subitems. This is a huge deficit in functionality. Due to the fact that subitems exist in Monday, there needs to be the ability to provide nested/group filtering similar to the below filter logic:

(STATUS is “Closed”) AND ((STATUS is “Closed”) AND (SUBSTATUS is “Closed”))

Without this ability, filtering is not complete and I am incapable of showing correct data in a single widget.

posts.author: tommy_H (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: Love to see the subitems just shows parent items. Hope I’m missing something?

posts.author: j.truong (Jennifer Truong)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s not very apparent, but the subitems do appear in the subitem filter. You just have to click on the arrow on the left to expand the subitems. The number on the right of the parent item indicates how many out of all subitems match your subitem filter condition.

For instance, I currently have a filter for subitems that have the status “Needs Attention.” My filter shows that out of all of the six subitems I have, three of them meet that condition. I can then expand my view and those three subitems will appear. Hope this helps.

monday.com subitem filter1107×371 34.2 KB

posts.author: tommy_H (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for clarification there but seems silly that they don’t expand to show them. Ties in with another simple feature request which is a toggle on/off for all subitems to open up. Having to click everyone to see what’s going on is time consuming. Hoping someone has a workaround

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Little update here: Filtering for dashboards and widgets is in gradual at the moment and soon it will be available to all accounts! So stay tuned if you still do not see it in your account!

posts.author: frossini (Fab Rossini)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the info Juliette! It works ! Keep up your great work

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: well done!!! this was a frequently asked feature in this community

posts.author: mstew1110 (Morgan)
posts.cookedHtml: Juliette, why do filters not work with “Show Item Summary on Parent Item” ? Why do exports not work when filtering by subitems?

posts.author: mstew1110 (Morgan)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb still have not received an answer here?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!

Sorry for the delay, I was away for a while. You can always ask other community members when you need some assistance. They are very helpful! Currently, the capabilities with this releases includes subitems being filtered by person, status, due date, etc. I just tested on my account and it does seem to work when summarizing parent items! I also tried exporting on my account and it seems to work. Can you elaborate on what the issue is? If you think its a problem with your account you can also reach out to support. Feel free to send images or videos!

posts.author: mstew1110 (Morgan)
posts.cookedHtml:
image1450×674 176 KB

Juliette, as you can see from the picture provided I filtered the subitems based on a specific date range. In this date range, we are tracking number of hours logged to a project (the parent item). When totalling these subitem hours to the parent after the filter it is not showing the filtered total, but the total of all subitems regardless of the filter. We would expect to see the “Total Hours” to equal 36 hours as per the filter, not the 862 hours as is being shown.

Is this something wrong with my account or am I doing something wrong here?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Morgan. Sorry for the delay. I was away for a few weeks but I did look into this and I believe filter you are using is not supposed to change the calculations of another item. If thats what you are looking for, maybe it would be better to use something like a chart here or a numbers widget. Another option maybe to compare the information side by side so you can see the breakdown with the filter applied. Let me know if this makes sense? You can also ask this question in the FAQ category and see if anyone else from the community has a good way around or solution for this. Hope this was helpful!

posts.author: mstew1110 (Morgan)
posts.cookedHtml: How does that make any sense? The column on the item is a total of the subitems hours column? If I filter the subitems, the total on the item column should update as well to reflect that. That’s the whole point of a filter… ?

posts.author: pjtgrizzly (Reid)
posts.cookedHtml: This still exists as of 5/1/2023 where filtered items still show aggregate of all subitems - including those filtered OUT on summary on parent item. frustrating! The summary numbers should show only those specific to what displays in the filter.

posts.author: mstew1110 (Morgan)
posts.cookedHtml: Yup, and for some reason they don’t think that is a problem. Most of their features are released with partial functionality and never resolved completely, it is extremely frustrating. We are getting to the size now where we are evaluating switching to Salesforce unfortunately. Their customer support and product development team is horrid.

posts.author: rlCFC (rLCFC)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Juliette,
It would be really great if the summary mirrored column calculations were filtered according to the board filter. As @mstew1110 pointed out, this is the logical and expected functionality of a filter. Without this functionality, subitem mirrored columns become worse than useless - they have erroneous information! It reduces the functionality of subitems as well. Is this feature going to be added to the Subitems workplan? Thank you!

posts.author: Thebancally (Mehmet)
posts.cookedHtml:
Subitem filters has been great until yesterday.It used to only shows items that is filtered.But now it shows items with 0 subitems.I want to see only items with filtered subitems again.I hope it will be fixed

subitem1920×1023 181 KB

posts.author: Thebancally (Mehmet)
posts.cookedHtml:
It has been fixed in 1 hour.What a speed.Thanks

It is broken again

posts.author: avp (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: Chiming in further support that subitem summary columns need to reflect the filters that are applied at the subitem level. This has especially poor impact on charts that display the summary column values - when you apply subitem filters, completely inaccurate data is presented.

posts.author: simpqlicious (s)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree.
It’s totally useless if the filter still keeps the summary of all sub-items that are not even shown anymore…

Please Monday, when will you fix this?

posts.author: JD182 (Jan Deichmann)
posts.cookedHtml:
I still can´t filter subitems in the App on a tablet or smartphone. Why is it possible on the browser, but not on the App?

You can use the browser on the tablet, but please let us change the layout more easily. No one needs an App, but a Browser Application, that you can use properbly on the Tablet or Smartphone.

Please give us some more opportunitys for the pocket devices! Would be great.

posts.author: lelandcurtis (Leland Curtis)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have the same issue as @mam7903 - subitem filters remove all items with 0 subitems.

Has this issue been resolved? I want to filter my table to include only items/subitems where item status is not “Done” AND subitem status is not “Done”.

Oddly, when i use the filter “subitem-status is not Done” it removes all items that have 0 subitems. This effectively means subitems filters can only be used on boards where EVERY item have a subitem, rather than only applying only to items that have subitems.

To me, it is logical for subitem filters to only apply if there is a subitem. The apparent default of assuming 0 subitems means it fails the filter and is removed is ridiculous.

I hoped “is empty” applied to a sub-item column might mean “no subitems” but it doesn’t.

Is there a workaround to this?

posts.author: Veronica_Reimer (Veronica)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m still looking for my subitem filters to show up properly in the mobile app… I schedule multiple crew members, and their calendar views do not show up properly in the app. They are always on the go/onsite, and it help immensely if these filters showed up properly in the app.
